evals.prompt: Convert this PDF to markdown: C:/tmp/sample-report.pdf
evals.expected_output: Runs scripts/convert.py on the PDF, writes C:/tmp/sample-report.md next to the source with the extracted text as clean markdown (headings, lists, and tables preserved where present), and reports the output path back to the user.

evals.prompt:
Turn this into a markdown file:

Project Kickoff Notes
Goals: ship v1 by Friday, get sign-off from client.
Risks - scope creep, late assets.
Next steps: 1) finalize wireframes 2) set up repo 3) book the review call
evals.expected_output: Recognizes this is raw pasted text (no file path), structures it into clean markdown with a heading and proper lists/sub-points, and writes it to a .md file (named by the user or a sensible default like C:/tmp/<slug>.md), then confirms the saved path. Does not require running the script.

evals.prompt: I have a Word doc at C:/tmp/proposal.docx - can you give me the markdown version?
evals.expected_output: Runs scripts/convert.py on the DOCX via markitdown, writes C:/tmp/proposal.md alongside the source, and reports the path. If markitdown is not installed, surfaces the install hint (pip install "markitdown[all]") and asks the user to install it.
